rationale: The citing paper uses Mace and Neslin (2004) repeatedly in core framing: it cites that study as resolving the postpromotion-dip existence debate and as a key prior analysis of dip determinants across products and stores. It is integrated into the literature review and hypothesis development (e.g., expected effects of budget share and promotion frequency), and its findings are revisited when interpreting results. This is more than a passing citation because the cited work helps define the specific dynamic-effect questions and moderator structure the new model addresses.
relation_description: The 2006 paper builds on Mace and Neslin's determinant-of-dips line of work by modeling immediate and dynamic price effects in a broader HB-ECM framework. It extends that prior approach from pre/postpromotion dip analysis to a wider set of elasticities, including cumulative promotion effects and regular-price dynamics across many categories and brands.
